core_meaning: Expresses quantity of uncountable food items using du/de la/de l'
primary_communicative_fn: Requesting or discussing food quantities in culinary contexts
when_to_use: When ordering, discussing meals, or describing food quantities
essential_vocabulary_slots: du, de la, de l', pain, viande, eau, crème, fromage
chunk_compatibility: Food/dining chunks (*Je prends du pain*, *Un verre de vin*)
key_forms: du pain / de la viande / de l'eau / de la crème
essential_vocabulary: du, de la, de l', pain, viande, eau, crème, fromage, beurre, confiture
why_it_matters: Partitive is mandatory with uncountable foods; omission or wrong article is ungrammatical
common_mistakes: Using 'un pain' for uncountable; keeping partitive in negation; using definite article

core_meaning: Indicates large quantity; always followed by 'de' then noun
when_to_use: When describing large amounts of countable or uncountable nouns
key_variations: beaucoup de temps (a lot of time — uncountable)
beaucoup d'amis (a lot of friends — countable with elision)
beaucoup d'eau (lots of water — uncountable with elision)
Il y a beaucoup de monde (There are many people)
essential_vocabulary_slots: beaucoup, de, d', temps, ami, eau, monde, livres, travail
common_learner_mistakes: Forgetting 'de' after 'beaucoup' (→ beaucoup de, not just beaucoup)
Omitting elision with vowels (d' before vowel)
chunk_compatibility: Quantity/abundance chunks (*J'ai beaucoup de travail*, *Beaucoup de gens*)
primary_function: Expressing significant quantities in speech and writing
key_forms: beaucoup de temps / beaucoup d'amis / beaucoup de travail
essential_vocabulary: beaucoup, de, d', temps, ami, eau, monde, livres, travail, amour
why_it_matters: Beaucoup is frequent; incorrect 'de' omission is immediately noticeable
common_mistakes: Omitting 'de'; forgetting elision; overusing with countables

when_to_use: When describing small/insufficient amounts of countable or uncountable nouns
essential_vocabulary_slots: peu, de, d', chance, argent, monde, livre, temps, courage
common_learner_mistakes: Confusing with 'un peu de' (a little — positive vs little — scarce)
Using in wrong semantic context (peu=lack, un peu=small amount)
chunk_compatibility: Scarcity chunks (*Peu de chance*, *Peu d'argent*, *Peu de temps*)
key_forms: peu de chance / peu d'argent / peu de monde / peu de temps
essential_vocabulary: peu, de, d', chance, argent, monde, livre, temps, courage, opportunité
why_it_matters: Distinction between 'peu' (scarce) and 'un peu' (small amount) is semantic
common_mistakes: Confusing 'peu' and 'un peu'; omitting 'de'; wrong context application

core_meaning: Indicates an indefinite plural number (more than two but unspecified)
primary_communicative_fn: Expressing plurality without specifying exact count
when_to_use: With plural nouns when describing multiple items without exact number
essential_vocabulary_slots: plusieurs, fois, jour, personne, solution, idée, heure, semaine
common_learner_mistakes: Adding 'de' after plusieurs (→ no preposition needed)
chunk_compatibility: Indefinite plural chunks (*Plusieurs fois*, *Plusieurs jours*, *Plusieurs personnes*)
primary_function: Indicating plural quantity without exact count
key_forms: plusieurs fois / plusieurs jours / plusieurs personnes
essential_vocabulary: plusieurs, fois, jour, personne, solution, idée, heure, semaine, mois
why_it_matters: Plusieurs is frequent for vague plurals; mandatory agreement with plural noun
common_mistakes: Using singular verb; adding 'de'; confusing with 'quelques' (few vs several)

core_meaning: Indicates a small indefinite number (few); always plural
primary_communicative_fn: Describing small quantity of countable items
when_to_use: With plural countable nouns for small unspecified amounts
essential_vocabulary_slots: quelques, minute, ami, idée, jour, personne, heure, exemple
common_learner_mistakes: Using with singular nouns (→ must be plural)
Omitting elision before vowel (quelques amis, not quelque amis)
chunk_compatibility: Small quantity chunks (*Quelques minutes*, *Quelques amis*, *Quelques jours*)
primary_function: Expressing small quantity of countable items
key_forms: quelques minutes / quelques amis / quelques idées / quelques jours
essential_vocabulary: quelques, minute, ami, idée, jour, personne, heure, exemple, raison
why_it_matters: Quelques is frequent in French; must always be plural; distinct from plusieurs
common_mistakes: Using with singular; confusing with 'plusieurs'; omitting plural marking

core_meaning: Indefinite pronoun meaning 'something'; always masculine, requires 'de' + adjective
primary_communicative_fn: Referring to unspecified entities or abstract concepts
when_to_use: When referring to something unidentified or abstract
essential_vocabulary_slots: quelque chose, de, important, nouveau, bizarre, spécial, beau
common_learner_mistakes: Placing adjective directly after (→ must use 'de' + adjective)
Using feminine or plural agreement (→ always masculine singular)
chunk_compatibility: Vague reference chunks (*quelque chose d'important*, *quelque chose de nouveau*)
primary_function: Indefinite pronoun reference to unspecified entities
key_forms: quelque chose d'important / quelque chose de nouveau
essential_vocabulary: quelque chose, de, important, nouveau, bizarre, spécial, beau, intéressant
why_it_matters: Quelque chose structure is fixed; deviation is immediately ungrammatical
common_mistakes: Omitting 'de'; direct adjective placement; incorrect gender agreement

core_meaning: Verbs and expressions requiring 'de' before noun in partitive sense
primary_communicative_fn: Expressing need, fear, desire regarding things
when_to_use: With verbs like avoir besoin de, avoir peur de, avoir envie de
essential_vocabulary_slots: besoin, peur, manger, boire, parler, avoir, envie, soif
common_learner_mistakes: Using definite article instead (→ avoir besoin du, wrong)
Confusion between partitive and definite usage
chunk_compatibility: Necessity/emotion chunks (*J'ai besoin de*, *J'ai peur de*, *J'ai envie de*)
primary_function: Expressing relationship between verb and noun via partitive
key_forms: avoir besoin de / avoir peur de / avoir envie de / parler de
essential_vocabulary: besoin, peur, envie, manger, boire, parler, avoir, soif, faim
why_it_matters: Partitive is mandatory after specific verbs; wrong article breaks meaning
common_mistakes: Using definite article (du/la/les); omitting 'de'; wrong construction entirely

core_meaning: Means 'too much/too many'; expresses excess quantity
when_to_use: When describing more than desired or necessary amount
essential_vocabulary_slots: trop, de, d', bruit, argent, problème, travail, temps, stress
common_learner_mistakes: Using trop instead of 'très' when meaning 'very'
chunk_compatibility: Excess quantity chunks (*Trop de travail*, *Trop d'argent*, *Trop de stress*)
key_forms: trop de bruit / trop d'argent / trop de travail
essential_vocabulary: trop, de, d', bruit, argent, problème, travail, temps, stress, dépense
why_it_matters: Trop/très distinction is semantic; trop implies 'too much' (negative), très = 'very'
common_mistakes: Using 'très' when 'trop' is correct; omitting 'de'; confusing with 'beaucoup'

primary_communicative_fn: Expressing adequacy or sufficiency of quantity
when_to_use: When describing sufficient or adequate amount
key_variations: Il y a assez d'espace (There's enough space)
essential_vocabulary_slots: assez, de, d', temps, eau, courage, espace, argent, nourriture
chunk_compatibility: Sufficiency chunks (*Assez de temps*, *Assez d'argent*, *C'est assez*)
key_forms: assez de temps / assez d'eau / assez de courage
essential_vocabulary: assez, de, d', temps, eau, courage, espace, argent, nourriture, calme
why_it_matters: Assez de is frequent; 'de' omission is immediately noticeable
common_mistakes: Omitting 'de'; using 'assez' alone with nouns; wrong placement

core_meaning: Negation changes partitive to 'de'; du/des becomes just 'de'
primary_communicative_fn: Expressing lack or absence via negative construction
when_to_use: In negative sentences with partitive or indefinite articles
key_variations: Je n'ai pas de travail (I don't have work)
Il ne boit pas d'alcool (He doesn't drink alcohol)
Nous n'avons pas de chance (We don't have luck)
Tu ne manges pas de viande? (You don't eat meat?)
essential_vocabulary_slots: ne, pas, de, d', travail, alcool, chance, viande, argent
common_learner_mistakes: Using partitive instead of 'de' in negation
chunk_compatibility: Negative statements (*Je n'ai pas de*, *Il ne boit pas d'*)
key_forms: Je n'ai pas de / Il ne boit pas d' / Nous n'avons pas de
essential_vocabulary: ne, pas, de, d', avoir, boire, manger, vouloir, pouvoir, travailler
why_it_matters: Partitive → de transformation is mandatory in negation; error breaks grammar
common_mistakes: Keeping partitive du/des in negative; forgetting 'ne'; omitting 'de'

core_meaning: Means 'most of'; requires article after: most + the + noun
primary_communicative_fn: Expressing majority or preponderance of entities
when_to_use: When describing majority portion of a group or mass
key_variations: la plupart des gens (most of the people)
la plupart du temps (most of the time)
la plupart des étudiants (most of the students)
la plupart d'entre eux (most of them)
essential_vocabulary_slots: la plupart, de, des, du, d', gens, temps, étudiant, entre
common_learner_mistakes: Forgetting that 'la plupart de' requires article before noun
chunk_compatibility: Majority chunks (*La plupart du temps*, *La plupart des gens*)
primary_function: Expressing majority quantity with required double article structure
key_forms: la plupart des gens / la plupart du temps / la plupart d'entre
essential_vocabulary: la plupart, de, des, du, d', gens, temps, étudiant, entre, jours
why_it_matters: Double article structure after 'la plupart de' is obligatory
common_mistakes: Omitting second article; using single article; wrong contraction form

core_meaning: Quantity expressions with beverages using container measure
primary_communicative_fn: Ordering or discussing beverages with measured quantities
when_to_use: When requesting or describing drinks measured by container
pattern: un verre de / une tasse de / une bouteille de + boisson
key_variations: un verre de vin (a glass of wine)
une tasse de café (a cup of coffee)
une bouteille d'eau (a bottle of water)
un verre d'eau (a glass of water)
essential_vocabulary_slots: verre, tasse, bouteille, de, vin, café, eau, lait
common_learner_mistakes: Using indefinite article instead of partitive (→ un verre du vin, wrong)
chunk_compatibility: Beverage chunks (*Un verre de vin*, *Une tasse de café*)
primary_function: Measured beverage expressions; ordering at café/restaurant
key_forms: un verre de vin / une tasse de café / une bouteille d'eau
essential_vocabulary: verre, tasse, bouteille, assiette, de, vin, café, eau, lait, jus
why_it_matters: Container + de + beverage is standard French ordering pattern
common_mistakes: Using indefinite article with beverage; omitting 'de'; wrong preposition

core_meaning: Means 'a little/some'; small positive amount (vs peu=scarce)
when_to_use: When describing small amount of something (positive sense)
key_variations: un peu de temps (a little time)
un peu de sucre (a little sugar)
un peu de patience (a little patience)
essential_vocabulary_slots: un peu, de, d', temps, sucre, eau, patience, lait, sel
common_learner_mistakes: Confusing with 'peu de' (little vs a little — semantic opposite)
chunk_compatibility: Small quantity chunks (*Un peu de temps*, *Un peu de sucre*, *Un peu de café*)
primary_function: Expressing small but positive quantity; softer than 'beaucoup'
key_forms: un peu de temps / un peu de sucre / un peu d'eau
essential_vocabulary: un peu, de, d', temps, sucre, eau, patience, lait, sel, café
why_it_matters: Semantic distinction 'peu' (scarce) vs 'un peu' (a little) is critical
common_mistakes: Confusing 'peu' and 'un peu'; omitting 'un'; wrong context

core_meaning: Expresses quantity of abstract concepts (courage, patience, hope, respect)
primary_communicative_fn: Discussing intangible qualities and virtues in quantified form
when_to_use: When describing amounts of abstract concepts/virtues/qualities
essential_vocabulary_slots: du, de la, de l', courage, patience, espoir, respect, amour
chunk_compatibility: Virtue/quality chunks (*Du courage*, *De la patience*, *De l'espoir*)
primary_function: Expressing quantity of intangible abstract concepts
key_forms: du courage / de la patience / de l'espoir / du respect
essential_vocabulary: du, de la, de l', courage, patience, espoir, respect, amour, beauté, liberté
why_it_matters: Abstract nouns are uncountable; partitive use is mandatory
common_mistakes: Using indefinite article; treating as countable; confusing with definite

core_meaning: Emphasis on remaining large quantity; combines 'still' with 'much'
primary_communicative_fn: Emphasizing abundance of remaining items despite consumption/use
when_to_use: When expressing that large quantity still remains/is available
key_variations: encore beaucoup de travail (still a lot of work)
encore beaucoup de temps (still lots of time)
encore beaucoup de possibilités (still many possibilities)
essential_vocabulary_slots: encore, beaucoup, de, d', travail, temps, ami, possibilité
chunk_compatibility: Emphasis chunks (*Encore beaucoup de travail*, *Encore beaucoup à faire*)
primary_function: Emphasizing remaining quantity or persistence of abundance
key_forms: encore beaucoup de travail / encore beaucoup de temps
essential_vocabulary: encore, beaucoup, de, d', travail, temps, ami, possibilité, raison
why_it_matters: Emphasis structure is fixed; appropriate for expressing remaining resources
common_mistakes: Doubling 'encore'; wrong word order; confusing with 'toujours beaucoup'
